name: Iregua Roman Road and Park Loop
name_original: Logroño, Parque del Iregua – circular por Vía Romana
description: This easy 14.3-mile mountain bike loop in Logroño's Parque del Iregua follows the historic Vía Romana, offering scenic river views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Logroño offer a diverse landscape for cyclists, characterized by the winding Ebro River, extensive vineyards, and lush forests of oaks, beeches, and pines. The region transitions from riverine scenery to rolling hills and more challenging mountain terrains, particularly towards the Sierra de Cantabria. This variety provides a wide network of trails suitable for different skill levels, from smooth paths to technically demanding descents. Best mountain bike trails around Logroño The most popular mountain bike trail is Iregua Roman Road and Park Loop, a 14.3 miles (23.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic riding through vineyards and along the Ebro River. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Cortijo Tower Loop, an easy 10.5 miles (16.9 km) path. This trail provides picturesque views through vineyards and along the Ebro River. Local mountain bikers also love the Los Peñuejos Rock Formations – Iregua Park loop from Logroño, a 30.8 miles (49.5 km) trail leading through rock formations and Iregua Park, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Mountain biking around Logroño is defined by river paths along the Ebro, routes through vineyards, and trails within forests of oaks and pines. The extensive network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more difficult climbs. The routes in Logroño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 4000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Logroño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Logroño?
A: Logroño offers an extensive network of over 170 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to a wide range of skill levels, from easy paths along the Ebro River to more challenging ascents through vineyards and forests.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Logroño?
A: The terrain around Logroño is incredibly diverse. You'll find smooth paths along the Ebro River, picturesque routes winding through vineyards, and trails within lush forests of oaks, beeches, and pines. Some areas also feature more challenging mountain terrains, particularly as you venture towards the Sierra de Cantabria, and even unique red dirt trails reminiscent of 'Spanish Utah'.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Logroño?
A: Logroño benefits from a generally pleasant climate, with temperatures typically ranging from 10 to 25 °C and infrequent rain. This makes much of the year suitable for mountain biking, though spring and autumn offer particularly comfortable conditions with mild weather and vibrant landscapes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Logroño?
A: Yes, Logroño has a good selection of trails perfect for beginners. You can find 18 easy routes, many of which offer scenic riding through vineyards and along the Ebro River. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994069238/el-cortijo-tower-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cortijo Tower Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 10.5-mile (16.9 km) path with picturesque views.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike routes around Logroño?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Logroño offers 64 difficult mountain bike routes. These trails often feature significant elevation gains and technical descents. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31511646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iregua Roman Road – Senda de las Dos Tetas loop from Logroño&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 33-mile (53.2 km) route with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the Logroño area?
A: Yes, many of the easier routes around Logroño are suitable for families. Trails along the Ebro River or through the vineyards, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994065622/iregua-roman-road-and-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iregua Roman Road and Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, provide scenic and relatively flat options for a pleasant family outing. The La Grajera Reservoir area also offers relaxed trails.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that are loop routes?
A: Absolutely. Many mountain bike trails around Logroño are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17553893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Peñuejos Rock Formations – Iregua Park loop from Logroño&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20056501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Winding Road Through Vineyards – El Cortijo Strong Tower loop from Logroño&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Logroño?
A: While mountain biking near Logroño, you can encounter several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a popular spot for relaxed rides, and you might also pass by other lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5150732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Prao de la Paul&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique geological formations and the scenic Ebro River, offering diverse landscapes to explore.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Logroño?
A: The mountain bike trails around Logroño are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from river paths and vineyards to forests, and the extensive network of trails that cater to all abilities. The pleasant climate is also a recurring highlight.

Q: Are there opportunities to see wildlife on the trails?
A: Yes, especially when venturing into forested areas or natural parks. The Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park, located south of Logroño, is known for its diverse wildlife, including foxes, wild boars, and deer. Even on trails closer to Logroño, you might spot various bird species and smaller animals, particularly in quieter sections along the river or through woodlands.

Q: Are there any historic routes I can cycle in the Logroño area?
A: Logroño is steeped in history, and you can certainly incorporate historic routes into your cycling. The renowned Camino de Santiago (Way of St. James) passes through Logroño, offering a unique blend of cultural exploration and outdoor activity. Additionally, the Ebro Natural Trail (GR 99) provides another long-distance cycling route along the river.
tips: This circuit is quite simple. It is circular and consists of only 16 kilometers. It is possible to do it in just over an hour and reach high speeds.

The Vía Romana del Iregua is a path marked out with purple and white markings that has been signposted along a path that runs parallel to the Iregua river. The original route of the old Roman road linked the towns of Vareia (Varea) and Numancia.

One of the large peri-urban parks in Logroño, next to the bed of the Iregua river, a tributary of the Ebro. Its layout is part of the GR-99 Long Distance path, also called the Ebro Natural Path. It forms part of the Logroño green belt, being very used for outdoor sports due to the connection with the La Ribera Park and the Ebro Park.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Logroño, Parque del Iregua – circular por Vía Romana route is categorized as an "Easy mountain bike ride." It's suitable for all skill levels and fitness levels, making it a great option for beginners and families looking for a pleasant ride along the Iregua river. A specific 4 km segment, "Via Romana Logroño - Alberite," is even noted as a "family friendly gravel path trail."

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied experience, primarily following a path parallel to the Iregua river. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, including asphalted paths, dirt tracks, and sections with riverside vegetation. Some segments, being part of a modern recreation of an ancient Roman road, may even reveal original paving stones. The Iregua Valley also features well-maintained forest tracks.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 23 km (14.3 miles) long and typically takes around 1 hour and 40 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Logroño, Parque del Iregua – circular por Vía Romana trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the route starts and ends in Logroño, making access convenient. Look for public parking options near the Parque del Iregua or Puente Madre, which are common starting points for trails in the area.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in the Parque del Iregua and along the Vía Romana are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in shared-use areas and to protect local wildlife. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for the Parque del Iregua or the Vía Romana?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Parque del Iregua or to ride the Vía Romana del Iregua trail. It's a public access route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable for much of the year. However, the Iregua Valley is particularly picturesque in autumn when the forests burst with vibrant colors from falling leaves. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and lush greenery. Summers can be warm, so consider early morning or late afternoon rides.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers scenic views as it hugs the Iregua river and passes through orchards and lush riverside vegetation. You'll also ride along the historic Iregua Roman Road, a modern recreation of an ancient path. The trail also passes through sections of the Iregua Park and near the Ecopark Singletracks.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Paseo del Iregua, Via Romana del Iregua, Camino Viejo de Alberite, Senda de la Chopera, and Senda del Iregua, among others, as you navigate the circular route.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like water or restrooms along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route itself doesn't guarantee specific facilities, being a circular route starting and ending in Logroño means you'll have access to amenities in the city. It's always advisable to carry enough water and snacks for your ride, especially as you venture further from urban areas.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Parque del Iregua?
faqs.answer: The Iregua Valley, with its extensive forests of oak, gall oak, and pine, and significant rock formations like the Castillo de Viguera, is important for birdlife. You might spot various bird species, and smaller mammals common to riverside and forest environments in the region.
